title: Ervatamia ophiorhizoides
taxonomy: Ervatamia ophiorhizoides is classified within the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. This species belongs to the genus Ervatamia, which comprises various species of flowering plants native to tropical regions. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Ervatamia ophiorhizoides is closely related to other plants like Vinca and Plumeria. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its stem structure, leaf shape, and flower arrangement. By understanding its classification, researchers can gain insights into the plant's evolutionary history and its relationships with other species within the Apocynaceae family.

intro: Ervatamia ophiorhizoides, also known as Ophiorrhiza root or Ervatamia root, is a type of plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family and is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine. The health benefits of Ervatamia ophiorhizoides include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ervatamia ophiorhizoides is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, as well as in modern pharmaceutical formulations, to treat a range of health conditions.
